english: whenever a death takes place, of caring for the cremation of the dead body (cf. čos-spún (chos spun)) Mil. and elsewh.; mdza-spún (mdza' spun) friend Cs. - 3. weft, woof in weaving.
1. sbst., also sbún-pa (sbun pa) B., C., sbur(-ma) (sbur ma) Dzl., Lal., chaff, husks etc. - 2. adj. a botanical term, description of the stalk of a plant Wdn.
pf. spubs (spubs), vb. a. to °búb-pa ('bub pa) to turn upside down.

also sku-spúr (sku spur), resp. for ro (ro), dead body, corpse, spur sbyáṅs-pa (spur sbyangs pa) C. to burn a dead body; spur-kań (spur khang) house for keeping dead bodies, or rather, in most cases, the place of cremation; spur-sgam (spur sgam) or sgrom (sgrom) coffin; spur-tal (spur thal) ashes of a dead body; spur - tsa (spur tsha) the salt for preparing a dead body; spur - šiṅ (spur shing) wood for burning a corpse.
vb. a. to °púr-ba ('phur ba), to make fly, to scare up, to let fly; dus spúr-ba (dus spur ba) to pass time quickly Cs.; stoṅ-spúr (stong spur) exaggeration, bombast Cs.

1. goods, merchandize, ware, spus ltá-ba (spus lta ba) to examine goods before purchase Cs.; *spus gyúr-če* (*spus gyur ce*) W., *pu gyúr-wa* (*pu gyur wa*) Cs. = *dal tsoṅ táṅ - če* (*dal tsong tang ce*). - 2. goodness, beauty, spús-čan (spus can), spus-gtsáṅ (spus gtsang), spus-bzáṅ (spus bzang), of fine appearance; spus-méd (spus med) ill-looking, unsightly. - 3. Sch.: for spos (spos).

turret, on a castle or gate W., (C. lčog (lcog)). High towers or steeples are seldom met with in Tibetan architecture; *peu gyú-čan riṅ-mo* (*pe'u gyu can ring mo*), mkár (mkhar) or káṅ-pa dgu-tóg (khang pa dgu thog) are the terms denoting such.
Cs.: n. of part of a cart.
gza-spén-pa (gza' spen pa) 1. the planet Saturn; the proper meaning is said to be a broom, hence the sign for it is somewhat resembling that implement Wdk. - 2. Saturday.
spén-šiṅ (spen shing), n. of a tree, prob. tamarisk; spen-bád (spen bad) a parapet, formed of the stems of tamarisk and raised on the roofs of monasteries.

vb. a. to °pel-ba ('phel ba), 1. to augment, to increase, nor (nor) the wealth Lex., bkra-šis (bkra shis) the welfare; rkaṅ-grós spél-ba (rkang gros spel ba) to breed cattle Dzl. and elsewh.; *spel-gyúd-la bor-če* (*spel rgyud la bor ce*) W. to keep cattle for breeding. - 2. to multiply (arithm.) Wdk. - 3. to spread, to propagate (news, secrets) Dzl. and elsewh.; more emphatically: spel rgyás-par (spel rgyas par), or sgróg-par byéd-pa (sgrog par byed pa) to blaze about Sch. - 4. to join, to put together, e.g. letters (almost = to spell); to mingle, to mix; spél-ma (spel ma) mixture, e.g. of prose and verse Cs.; acc. to Was. however, couplets, similar both as to metre and contents; composition, combination, gser gýu spél-mai kri (gser g.yu spel ma'i khri) a chair of gold and turkoises Pth.; spél-mai nor (spel ma'i nor) mixed goods Cs.; spél-gos (spel gos) clothes of various colours Cs.; spel-tsig (spel tshig) Sch.: a combination of verses, poetry (?); spél-mar byéd-pa (spel mar byed pa) to mix Lex.

summit of a mountain, brag-dmar spo-mtó-nas (brag dmar spo mtho nas) from the height of Bragmar Mil.; rdo-rje-gdán-gyi spó-la (rdo rje gdan gyi spo la) on the top of Gayā Pth.; spó-bo (spo bo) 1. (top, point =) bud Ts. 2. district to the east of Lhasa Glr.
1. bullock C. - 2. n. of a village in Panyul.

pf. and imp. spos (spos), vb. a. to °pó-ba ('pho ba), to alter, to change; with and without gnas (gnas) (W. *sa* (*sa*)): to change the place (of residence), to remove, to shift; also to transpose, transplant; miṅ spó-ba (ming spo ba) to change the name Mil.; gos spó-ba (gos spo ba) to change one's dress; mgo-lús (mgo lus) v. mgo (mgo) extr.; to remove (an officer) to another station; to dismiss (a servant), W., also B. frq.; gžan mkás-pa yód-na spós-pa bzaṅ (gzhan mkhas pa yod na spos pa bzang) if another skilful (physician) is to be had, it will be better to dismiss (the present one); to alter, to mend, to correct W.; spó-sa (spo sa) a place newly occupied by nomads Sch.
pf. spags (spags), fut. spag (spag), to remove and to bring near by turns Cs.; Lexx. w.e. -
